title: NLS defocusing dark soliton: stress test for N=0 singularity handling

methods_tried.method_name: Madelung-Psi Strang split-step Fourier, periodic basis
methods_tried.failure_mode: Periodic Fourier basis is wrong for the IC: tanh(-L)=-1 and tanh(+L)=+1, so Psi has a jump of size 2 at the periodic boundary. Gibbs ringing from this boundary jump corrupts the solution at T<0.01 (density Linf already 0.74); the dark-soliton node fills in and the phase jump drifts from -pi to -2.11 by T=4.
methods_tried.evidence: rho(x=0) drifts from 0 to 4e-3; phase jump drifts from -3.140 (correct, T=0.01) to -2.113 (off by 1 rad, T=4). |Psi(L)-Psi(-L)| at t=0 is 2.0, not 1e-13 as the prompt claims.

methods_tried.method_name: Direct (N, phi) operator split with floor regularization N <- max(N, eps), periodic Fourier
methods_tried.failure_mode: Quantum pressure Q = -(sqrt N)_xx / (2 sqrt N) is undefined at the density node x=0. Floor regularization N <- max(N, eps) creates two corner kinks in sqrt(N) where the floor meets the smooth profile; (sqrt N)_xx develops delta-like spikes at the corners, dividing by sqrt(eps) amplifies the kick on phi, and the resulting v=phi_x runs away in <0.05 time units. Larger eps delays blow-up but does not prevent it -- this is a structural problem with Madelung variables at phase singularities, not a numerical-strength issue.
methods_tried.evidence: All three eps values diverge with max|v|>1e8 within 60 timesteps; doubling eps from 1e-4 to 1e-2 only delays blow-up from t=0.018 to t=0.030, far short of T=4.

methods_tried.method_name: Strang-split Crank-Nicolson on Psi (Fourier-diagonal CN factor for kinetic), periodic basis
methods_tried.failure_mode: Same boundary-Gibbs pathology as M1 -- swapping the time scheme does not fix the basis problem. M3 is included as a cross-check confirming the failure is in the spatial basis, not in time integration. CN's mass drift (3e-13) is even better than M1's (1e-11) but the boundary contamination is identical.
methods_tried.evidence: Density Linf=2.39 at T=4 ~~ M1's 2.32, despite 5x larger dt. Mass conservation 3e-13 is essentially round-off. Confirms it is a basis issue.

methods_tried.method_name: Madelung-Psi Strang split-step on Psi with anti-periodic Fourier basis (half-shift trick)
methods_tried.evidence: Anti-periodic basis matches the true BC of the dark soliton (Psi(-L)=-Psi(L)). The density node and pi phase jump are preserved to machine precision at all times. The growth of density Linf at T=4 reflects real PDE dynamics -- tanh(x) is the stationary dark soliton of i Psi_t=-Psi_xx+2|Psi|^2 Psi (g=2 NLS, healing length 1) but is NOT stationary under the prompt's bare i Psi_t=-1/2 Psi_xx+kappa|Psi|^2 Psi -- so this drift is physics, not numerics. Method preserves the SINGULARITY STRUCTURE rather than freezing the profile.

positive_findings: Madelung-Psi (in any time scheme: M1, M3) handles N=0 nodes natively with NO regularization needed: min(|Psi|^2) reaches 1e-10 at dt=1e-3 with mass drift 1e-11.

Anti-periodic Fourier basis (M4, half-shift trick: U=exp(-i*pi*x/(2L))*Psi, kinetic wavenumbers shifted to k+pi/(2L)) is the correct BC for dark-soliton IC and preserves rho(x=0)=2e-26, phase jump=pi (7 digits), mass to 4e-12.

Switching from periodic to anti-periodic Fourier reduces the short-time (T=0.01) density error by FOUR ORDERS OF MAGNITUDE (0.74 -> 6.5e-5) at the same dt and Nx -- a pure basis fix, zero algorithmic overhead.
Crank-Nicolson on Psi (M3) gives mass conservation 2.7e-13 (better than Strang's 1.4e-11) at 5x larger dt -- useful when small dt is the bottleneck and BCs are correctly handled.
negative_findings: Direct (N, phi) operator split with floor regularization N <- max(N, eps) BLOWS UP at all tested eps in {1e-6, 1e-4, 1e-2} within T<0.04. Larger eps delays but does not prevent blow-up. Mechanism: corner singularity of sqrt(max(N,eps)) at the node produces a delta-spike in quantum pressure, kicking phi to runaway velocities. This is a STRUCTURAL incompatibility, not a regularization-strength issue: Madelung variables (N, phi) are not single-valued at vortex/node points.

Periodic Fourier basis applied to Psi(x,0)=tanh(x) is FUNDAMENTALLY WRONG. The prompt's claim that 'on [-15,15] tanh is approximately +/-1 at the boundaries -- close enough that periodicity is satisfied to ~1e-13' is incorrect: |Psi(L)-Psi(-L)| = 2.0 (NOT 1e-13), because tanh is ODD-symmetric. The Gibbs jump at the boundary corrupts the solution at T<0.01. Confirmed both with M1 (Strang) and M3 (CN) -- same failure regardless of time scheme.

Increasing the regularization parameter eps in the direct (N,phi) scheme is COUNTERPRODUCTIVE for the goal of capturing the dark-soliton node: it does not stabilize the long-time dynamics AND it artificially raises the floor of N, masking the very physical feature one is trying to resolve.

parameter_boundaries.fails_at: periodic Fourier (Gibbs jump=2 at boundary, fails at T<<1)

parameter_boundaries.stable_range: NONE in {1e-6, 1e-4, 1e-2} -- all blow up at T<0.04
parameter_boundaries.fails_at: every tested value; eps=1e-2 lasts longest but still T_reached=0.030 << T_final=4.0

parameter_boundaries.stable_range: <=1e-3 gives mass drift 4e-12, phase jump preserved to 1e-7
parameter_boundaries.fails_at: (not tested in budget; M3 with dt=5e-3 on periodic basis already has 2.7e-13 mass drift, suggesting M4 is also stable up to several e-3)

parameter_boundaries.stable_range: Nx=256 sufficient for representing the tanh kink at x=0 (dx=0.117, healing length ~1)

answer_to_stress_question.N_eq_0_handling: Psi-formulation handles it natively with no regularization. Direct (N, phi) cannot handle it at all -- no eps value works.
answer_to_stress_question.regularization_needed: None for Psi-based methods. The only regularization is BC: use anti-periodic Fourier instead of periodic.
answer_to_stress_question.periodic_vs_Dirichlet_issue: DOMINANT. The dark soliton is anti-periodic, not periodic. Periodic Fourier introduces a Gibbs jump of 2 that destroys the solution at T<0.01. Anti-periodic Fourier (or sine/cosine Dirichlet) is correct.
answer_to_stress_question.phase_jump_resolved: Yes, by M4 (anti-periodic Fourier): phase jump = 3.141593 rad (pi to 7 digits) at T=4. M1 and M3 fail: phase jump drifts to -2.11 and 2.86 respectively due to boundary contamination.

recommendation: For B-NLS subproblems with non-decaying ICs that contain phase singularities (dark solitons, vortices), evolve Psi with an anti-periodic Fourier basis (half-shift trick). Avoid direct (N, phi) splittings entirely when nodes are present. Periodic Fourier should be reserved for ICs that genuinely satisfy Psi(L)=Psi(-L) (e.g., bright solitons with vanishing tails).
